---
title: "Phase C — Interactive simulator"
subtitle: "Live Monte Carlo on parameters of your choosing"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    theme: cosmo
    embed-resources: false
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .phase-c-controls { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(260px, 1fr)); gap: 1rem 1.5rem; padding: 1rem; border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa; }
  .phase-c-controls .group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 0.35rem; }
  .phase-c-controls .group h4 { margin: 0 0 0.25rem; font-size: 0.95rem; color: #333; }
  .phase-c-derived { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)); gap: 0.75rem; margin: 1rem 0; }
  .phase-c-derived .card { border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 6px; padding: 0.6rem 0.75rem; background: #fff; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .label { font-size: 0.8rem; color: #555; }
  .phase-c-derived .card .value { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 1.1rem; }
  .callout-note-lite { border-left: 3px solid #4c78a8; background: #f3f7fb; padding: 0.6rem 0.9rem; margin: 0.8rem 0; font-size: 0.95rem; }

  /* --- Sticky controls layout ---------------------------------------- */
  /* Two-column grid: inputs pinned on the left, charts on the right.
     Falls back to a single column on narrow screens. */
  @media (min-width: 1100px) {
    .pc-layout { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 320px minmax(0, 1fr); gap: 1.5rem; align-items: start; }
    .pc-controls-pane {
      position: sticky; top: 0.75rem;
      max-height: calc(100vh - 1.5rem);
      overflow-y: auto;
      padding: 0.75rem 1rem 1rem;
      border: 1px solid #ddd; border-radius: 8px; background: #fafafa;
      font-size: 0.9rem;
    }
    .pc-controls-pane h2 { font-size: 1.05rem; margin: 0.25rem 0 0.5rem; }
    .pc-controls-pane h3 { font-size: 0.95rem; margin: 0.9rem 0 0.4rem; color: #333; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.04em; border-bottom: 1px solid #e0e0e0; padding-bottom: 0.2rem; }
    .pc-controls-pane p { font-size: 0.82rem; color: #555; margin: 0.25rem 0 0.6rem; }
    .pc-controls-pane form { width: 100%; }
    .pc-controls-pane label { font-size: 0.82rem; }
    .pc-controls-pane input[type="range"] { width: 100%; }
  }
  /* Compact "live readout" strip inside the sticky pane. */
  .pc-readout { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 0.3rem 0.6rem; margin: 0.4rem 0 0.2rem; font-size: 0.8rem; }
  .pc-readout .k { color: #555; }
  .pc-readout .v { font-family: ui-monospace, monospace; text-align: right; }
  /* Let Plot/Inputs.table SVGs shrink to fit the constrained content pane. */
  .pc-content-pane svg, .pc-content-pane figure { max-width: 100%; }
</style>
```

## Starting Phase C

Phase B cross-checked the closed-form §1–§5 quantities against Monte Carlo
and exposed a **pre-baked** sweep over $(\alpha, \mu, \sigma)$ through the
Observable notebook. Phase C extends the modelling menu — jump diffusion,
regime switching, Poisson retirements, historical calibration, dynamic
hedging — and the first piece of scaffolding it needs is **a way to run the
simulator live on parameters of our choosing**, not just a pre-computed grid.

This page is that scaffolding. Every slider below triggers a fresh Monte
Carlo in the browser (no server round-trip, no artifact regeneration). The
baseline price process is the same GBM used in Phase B; subsequent Phase C
iterations will swap in the richer dynamics behind the same UI.

::: {.callout-note-lite}
**What changes vs. Phase B.** The pre-purchase hedge is now specified by
the **raw inventory state** — a number of kVCM tokens and their USD cost
basis — instead of the ratio $\alpha$. This removes the Phase B assumption
that the tokens were bought today at $S_0$: you can seed the simulation
with an inventory that was acquired earlier at a different price. The
Phase B $(\alpha = k / (N \cdot P),\; \text{basis} = \alpha \cdot N \cdot P \cdot S_0)$ parametrisation
is a special case of the new one.
:::

::: {.pc-layout}

::: {.pc-controls-pane}
## Controls

### Price process

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof pi0 = Inputs.range([0, 500], { step: 1, value: 125, label: "π₀ (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof S0 = Inputs.range([0.005, 1], { step: 0.005, value: 0.07, label: "S₀ (USD / kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mu = Inputs.range([-0.5, 0.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0.05, label: "drift μ (annualised)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sigma2 = Inputs.range([0, 2.5], { step: 0.005, value: 0.25, label: "variance σ² (annualised)" })
```

### Demand & inventory

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof lambdaDay = Inputs.range([0, 50], { step: 0.1, value: 1, label: "retirements (tonnes / day)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof kPre = Inputs.range([0, 2000000], { step: 1000, value: 300000, label: "initial inventory (kVCM)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cBasis = Inputs.range([0, 1000000], { step: 100, value: 21000, label: "inventory basis (USD, total)" })
```

### Contract

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Tdays = Inputs.range([30, 1095], { step: 5, value: 365, label: "horizon T (days)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof Q = Inputs.range([0, 1000], { step: 0.5, value: 180, label: "fixed quote Q (USD / tonne)" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof fee = Inputs.range([0, 1], { step: 0.01, value: 0.4, label: "fee rate f" })
```

### Monte Carlo

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nPaths = Inputs.select([2000, 5000, 10000, 20000, 40000], { value: 5000, label: "paths" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof nSteps = Inputs.select([50, 100, 200, 365], { value: 100, label: "steps / path" })
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof seed = Inputs.range([1, 9999], { step: 1, value: 42, label: "PRNG seed" })
```

### Live readout

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="pc-readout">
  <div class="k">P = π₀ / S₀</div><div class="v">${derived.P.toFixed(3)}</div>
  <div class="k">σ</div><div class="v">${derived.sigma.toFixed(3)}</div>
  <div class="k">T (yr)</div><div class="v">${derived.T.toFixed(2)}</div>
  <div class="k">N (tonnes)</div><div class="v">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div>
  <div class="k">coverage</div><div class="v">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div>
  <div class="k">Q* (USD/t)</div><div class="v">${derived.QStar.toFixed(2)}</div>
</div>`
```

:::

::: {.pc-content-pane}

Drift $\mu$ and variance $\sigma^2$ are annualised. The retirement rate
$\lambda$ is specified per calendar day; internally we use a year as the
time unit, so $\lambda_{\mathrm{yr}} = 365 \cdot \lambda_{\mathrm{day}}$.
Defaults are set so that $Q$ tracks the Phase B fee-equivalent quote at
$t = 0$; override to study off-market pricing.

## Derived quantities

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
derived = {
  const sigma = Math.sqrt(sigma2);
  const P = pi0 / S0;
  const T = Tdays / 365;
  const lambda = lambdaDay * 365;
  const N = lambda * T;
  const tokensNeeded = N * P;
  const coverageDays = (lambda > 0 && P > 0) ? kPre / (lambda * P) * 365 : Infinity;
  const coverageFrac = tokensNeeded > 0 ? Math.min(1, kPre / tokensNeeded) : 0;
  const impliedCost = kPre > 0 ? cBasis / kPre : 0;
  const QStarRatio = expm1OverX(mu * T);
  const QStar = (1 + fee) * P * S0 * QStarRatio;
  return { sigma, P, T, lambda, N, tokensNeeded, coverageDays, coverageFrac, impliedCost, QStar };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">σ (volatility)</div><div class="value">${derived.sigma.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">P = π₀ / S₀ (kVCM / tonne)</div><div class="value">${derived.P.toFixed(4)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">T (years)</div><div class="value">${derived.T.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">N = λ·T (tonnes over horizon)</div><div class="value">${derived.N.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">tokens needed N·P</div><div class="value">${derived.tokensNeeded.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage from inventory</div><div class="value">${(derived.coverageFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">inventory runs out at (days)</div><div class="value">${isFinite(derived.coverageDays) ? Math.min(derived.coverageDays, Tdays).toFixed(1) : "∞"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">implied basis per token</div><div class="value">${kPre > 0 ? "$" + derived.impliedCost.toFixed(3) : "—"}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">break-even Q* (USD / tonne)</div><div class="value">${derived.QStar.toFixed(3)}</div></div>
</div>`
```

::: {.callout-note-lite}
**Interpreting "coverage".** If the pre-purchased inventory covers 100% of
the horizon's token demand, the book is fully matched (§3a-style). Below
100%, the operator runs out of inventory part-way through the horizon and
has to buy the remainder back-to-back at spot — that is where the left-tail
risk appears. Above 100% (over-hedged) the excess tokens are marked-to-
market at $S_T$ at the horizon.
:::

## Core simulator

All cells below are pure JavaScript running in the browser — no server, no
pre-computed JSON. The simulator is a direct port of `src/rng.ts`,
`src/gbm.ts`, `src/moments.ts`, and `src/risk.ts`. Keeping it inline lets
readers audit every calculation in one place.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function makeRng(seedVal) {
  let state = seedVal >>> 0;
  let cached = null;
  const uniform = () => {
    state = (state + 0x6d2b79f5) >>> 0;
    let t = state;
    t = Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 15), t | 1);
    t ^= t + Math.imul(t ^ (t >>> 7), t | 61);
    const u = ((t ^ (t >>> 14)) >>> 0) / 4294967296;
    return u === 0 ? 1 / 4294967296 : u;
  };
  const normal = () => {
    if (cached !== null) {
      const z = cached;
      cached = null;
      return z;
    }
    const u1 = uniform();
    const u2 = uniform();
    const r = Math.sqrt(-2 * Math.log(u1));
    const th = 2 * Math.PI * u2;
    cached = r * Math.sin(th);
    return r * Math.cos(th);
  };
  return { uniform, normal };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function expm1OverX(x) {
  if (x === 0) return 1;
  if (Math.abs(x) < 1e-8) return 1 + x / 2 + (x * x) / 6;
  return Math.expm1(x) / x;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function quantileSorted(sorted, q) {
  const n = sorted.length;
  if (n === 0) return NaN;
  if (n === 1) return sorted[0];
  const pos = q * (n - 1);
  const lo = Math.floor(pos);
  const hi = Math.ceil(pos);
  if (lo === hi) return sorted[lo];
  const frac = pos - lo;
  return sorted[lo] * (1 - frac) + sorted[hi] * frac;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function tailMean(sortedAscending, q) {
  const n = sortedAscending.length;
  const k = Math.max(1, Math.ceil((1 - q) * n));
  let s = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < k; i++) s += sortedAscending[i];
  return s / k;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function simulate(inputs) {
  const { S0, mu, sigma, P, lambda, T, Q, fee, kPre, cBasis, nPaths, nSteps, seed } = inputs;
  const rng = makeRng(seed);
  const dt = T / nSteps;
  const drift = (mu - 0.5 * sigma * sigma) * dt;
  const diffusion = sigma * Math.sqrt(dt);
  const N = lambda * T;
  // Fraction of horizon over which inventory lasts, clamped to [0, 1].
  // Retirements are a constant flow λ, so `kPre` tokens cover
  // τ = kPre / (λ P) years of demand.
  const tauFrac = (lambda > 0 && P > 0)
    ? Math.min(1, kPre / (lambda * P * T))
    : 1;
  const tokensUsedInternal = Math.min(kPre, lambda * T * P);
  const tokensLeftover = Math.max(0, kPre - lambda * T * P);
  // When inventory covers the whole horizon, the tail integral range [τT, T]
  // has zero length; park tailStartStep beyond the loop so no node ever
  // contributes. (Math.ceil(1 · nSteps) = nSteps would otherwise add
  // 0.5·S_T·dt from the single collapsed endpoint.)
  // Snap tauFrac onto the integration grid and treat "only the endpoint
  // falls in [τT, T]" as an empty range — otherwise the trapezoid rule
  // would contribute 0.5·S_T·dt for a zero-length interval.
  const tailStartRaw = Math.ceil(tauFrac * nSteps);
  const tailStartStep = tailStartRaw >= nSteps ? nSteps + 1 : tailStartRaw;

  const feeSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const principalSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const b2bSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const ITSamples = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const terminalS = new Float64Array(nPaths);
  const keepPaths = Math.min(20, nPaths);
  const sampledPaths = [];

  for (let i = 0; i < nPaths; i++) {
    // Trapezoid accumulators: full-horizon I_T and uncovered-tail integral
    // ∫_{τ T}^{T} S_t dt. We stitch the tail integral out of the same
    // trajectory so it uses the same realisations as I_T.
    let S = S0;
    let IT = 0;
    let tailInt = 0;
    const path = (i < keepPaths) ? new Float64Array(nSteps + 1) : null;
    if (path) path[0] = S;

    // Trapezoid rule: weight endpoints by 1/2.
    IT += 0.5 * S;
    if (tailStartStep === 0) tailInt += 0.5 * S;

    for (let k = 1; k <= nSteps; k++) {
      const z = rng.normal();
      S = S * Math.exp(drift + diffusion * z);
      if (path) path[k] = S;
      const w = (k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
      IT += w * S;
      if (k >= tailStartStep) {
        const wt = (k === tailStartStep || k === nSteps) ? 0.5 : 1;
        tailInt += wt * S;
      }
    }
    IT *= dt;
    tailInt *= dt;
    terminalS[i] = S;
    ITSamples[i] = IT;

    // Fee book: R_fee = f · P · λ · I_T.
    feeSamples[i] = fee * P * lambda * IT;

    // Back-to-back principal book (no pre-purchase): Π = Q·N − P·λ·I_T.
    b2bSamples[i] = Q * N - P * lambda * IT;

    // Custom principal with initial inventory:
    //   Π = Q·N − C_basis − P·λ·∫_{τT}^{T} S_t dt + leftover · S_T.
    // The cost basis is a sunk cost at t=0; uncovered retirements after
    // inventory exhaustion are bought at spot; excess tokens mark-to-market
    // at horizon.
    principalSamples[i] =
      Q * N - cBasis - P * lambda * tailInt + tokensLeftover * S;

    if (path) sampledPaths.push(path);
  }

  return {
    feeSamples,
    principalSamples,
    b2bSamples,
    ITSamples,
    terminalS,
    sampledPaths,
    tauFrac,
    tokensUsedInternal,
    tokensLeftover,
    N,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
function summarise(samples) {
  const n = samples.length;
  let sum = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) sum += samples[i];
  const mean = sum / n;
  let sse = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
    const d = samples[i] - mean;
    sse += d * d;
  }
  const variance = n > 1 ? sse / (n - 1) : 0;
  const sd = Math.sqrt(variance);
  const stderr = sd / Math.sqrt(n);
  const sorted = Float64Array.from(samples); sorted.sort();
  const var95 = -quantileSorted(sorted, 0.05);
  const var99 = -quantileSorted(sorted, 0.01);
  const cvar95 = -tailMean(sorted, 0.95);
  const cvar99 = -tailMean(sorted, 0.99);
  let losses = 0;
  for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) if (samples[i] < 0) losses++;
  const probLoss = losses / n;
  const sharpe = sd > 0 ? mean / sd : null;
  return { mean, sd, stderr, ci95: 1.96 * stderr, var95, var99, cvar95, cvar99, probLoss, sharpe };
}
```

## Monte Carlo run

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
run = {
  const inputs = {
    S0, mu, sigma: derived.sigma, P: derived.P,
    lambda: derived.lambda, T: derived.T,
    Q, fee, kPre, cBasis,
    nPaths, nSteps, seed,
  };
  const t0 = performance.now();
  const raw = simulate(inputs);
  const elapsedMs = performance.now() - t0;
  return {
    inputs,
    ...raw,
    fee: summarise(raw.feeSamples),
    principal: summarise(raw.principalSamples),
    b2b: summarise(raw.b2bSamples),
    elapsedMs,
  };
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div class="phase-c-derived">
  <div class="card"><div class="label">paths simulated</div><div class="value">${nPaths.toLocaleString()}</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">wall time</div><div class="value">${run.elapsedMs.toFixed(1)} ms</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">coverage this run</div><div class="value">${(run.tauFrac * 100).toFixed(1)}%</div></div>
  <div class="card"><div class="label">leftover tokens at T</div><div class="value">${run.tokensLeftover.toLocaleString(undefined, {maximumFractionDigits: 0})}</div></div>
</div>`
```

### Summary statistics

Three books are simulated on each draw:

- **Fee** — $R_{\mathrm{fee}} = f \cdot P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$, revenue only.
- **Principal (back-to-back)** — $\Pi_{\mathrm{b2b}} = Q \cdot N - P \cdot \lambda \cdot I_T$. No pre-purchase, for reference.
- **Principal (custom inventory)** — $\Pi_{\mathrm{cust}} = Q \cdot N - C_{\mathrm{basis}} - P \cdot \lambda \int_{\tau T}^{T} S_t\,dt + k_{\mathrm{left}} \cdot S_T$, the active slider model.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
summaryRows = [
  { book: "fee", ...run.fee },
  { book: "principal (custom)", ...run.principal },
  { book: "principal (back-to-back)", ...run.b2b },
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Inputs.table(summaryRows, {
  columns: ["book","mean","sd","ci95","var95","var99","cvar95","cvar99","probLoss","sharpe"],
  header: {
    book: "book",
    mean: "E[Π] (mc)",
    sd: "SD",
    ci95: "±CI95",
    var95: "VaR95",
    var99: "VaR99",
    cvar95: "CVaR95",
    cvar99: "CVaR99",
    probLoss: "P[Π<0]",
    sharpe: "Sharpe",
  },
  format: {
    mean: x => x.toFixed(2),
    sd: x => x.toFixed(2),
    ci95: x => "±" + x.toFixed(2),
    var95: x => x.toFixed(2),
    var99: x => x.toFixed(2),
    cvar95: x => x.toFixed(2),
    cvar99: x => x.toFixed(2),
    probLoss: x => x.toFixed(3),
    sharpe: x => x === null ? "—" : x.toFixed(3),
  }
})
```

### P&L distributions

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pnlRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const push = (label, arr) => {
    for (let i = 0; i < arr.length; i++) rows.push({ book: label, pnl: arr[i] });
  };
  // Subsample for plotting responsiveness.
  const stride = Math.max(1, Math.floor(run.feeSamples.length / 2500));
  for (let i = 0; i < run.feeSamples.length; i += stride) {
    rows.push({ book: "fee", pnl: run.feeSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (custom)", pnl: run.principalSamples[i] });
    rows.push({ book: "principal (back-to-back)", pnl: run.b2bSamples[i] });
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 440,
  marginLeft: 80,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "terminal P&L (USD)", tickFormat: "~s" },
  y: { label: "empirical density" },
  fy: { label: "book" },
  color: { legend: true, domain: ["fee","principal (custom)","principal (back-to-back)"] },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.ruleX([0], { stroke: "#888", strokeDasharray: "2 4" }),
    Plot.rectY(pnlRecords, Plot.binX({ y: "proportion" }, { x: "pnl", fill: "book", fy: "book", thresholds: 50 })),
    Plot.ruleX([
      { book: "fee", x: run.fee.mean },
      { book: "principal (custom)", x: run.principal.mean },
      { book: "principal (back-to-back)", x: run.b2b.mean },
    ], { x: "x", fy: "book", stroke: "black", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Solid black: MC-estimated $\mathbb{E}[\Pi]$. Dashed grey: $\Pi = 0$. The
custom-principal panel collapses toward a spike as coverage approaches
100% — that's the §3a matched book limit._

### Sample price paths and inventory decay

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
pathRecords = {
  const rows = [];
  const paths = run.sampledPaths;
  const nS = run.inputs.nSteps;
  for (let idx = 0; idx < paths.length; idx++) {
    const path = paths[idx];
    for (let k = 0; k <= nS; k++) {
      rows.push({
        tDays: (k / nS) * Tdays,
        S: path[k],
        id: idx,
      });
    }
  }
  return rows;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
Plot.plot({
  width: 900,
  height: 340,
  marginLeft: 70,
  marginBottom: 50,
  grid: true,
  x: { label: "t (days)", domain: [0, Tdays] },
  y: { label: "kVCM spot S_t (USD)", zero: true, tickFormat: "~s" },
  marks: [
    Plot.ruleY([0]),
    Plot.line(pathRecords, { x: "tDays", y: "S", z: "id", stroke: "#888", strokeOpacity: 0.35 }),
    Plot.line(pathRecords.filter(r => r.id === 0), { x: "tDays", y: "S", stroke: "#c94", strokeWidth: 2 }),
    Plot.ruleX([run.tauFrac * Tdays], { stroke: "#2a7", strokeDasharray: "4 3", strokeWidth: 2 }),
  ]
})
```

_Grey: sampled GBM trajectories of the kVCM spot. Orange: first path
highlighted. Green dashed rule: time at which the pre-purchased inventory
is exhausted — to the right of this line, retirements are sourced at
spot._

:::

:::

## Notes

- **Performance.** Each slider change re-runs the simulator. At 5,000
  paths × 100 steps the wall time reported above should be well under a
  second on a typical laptop. Increase `Monte Carlo paths` for tighter
  tails; decrease for responsiveness while exploring.
- **Reproducibility.** The PRNG is the same seeded Mulberry32 as Phase B,
  so a given `(seed, inputs)` pair is deterministic across reloads.
- **What is still Phase B under the hood.** GBM dynamics and deterministic
  retirement flow. Phase C iterations will replace these with
  jump-diffusion, regime switching, and Poisson demand while keeping the
  same UI and book definitions.
- **What is new beyond Phase B.** The principal book is parametrised by
  the raw inventory state (tokens + cost basis), not by a ratio $\alpha$.
  This allows seeding with inventory bought previously at a price different
  from $S_0$ — a prerequisite for the calibration work later in Phase C.
